title: C2: HTTPS Beaconing Drilldown
description: Shows repeated HTTPS callbacks to suspicious destinations across Sysmon network, VCN Flow, and Network Firewall telemetry. Port 443 rows with high connection counts are the main C2 beaconing evidence.
query: (((('Log Source' = 'SOC Sysmon Network Logs' or 'Log Source' = 'Windows Sysmon Operational Logs' or 'Log Source' = 'SOC Windows Sysmon Logs') and 'Event ID' = '3' and ('Destination IP' = '198.51.100.77' or 'Destination Hostname' like '*duckdns.org' or 'Destination Hostname' like '*malware.xyz' or 'Destination Hostname' like '*attacker.top' or 'Destination Hostname' like '*cobalt.example.com' or 'Destination Port' = 4443 or 'Destination Port' = 8443)) or (('Log Source' = 'SOC VCN Flow Logs' or 'Log Source' = 'SOC Network Firewall Logs') and ('Destination IP' = '198.51.100.77' or 'Threat Name' = 'Suspicious Data Exfiltration'))) and 'Destination Port' = 443) | stats count as Connections, unique('Destination Hostname') as Hostnames, unique('Bytes Sent') as BytesSent by 'Log Source', 'Source IP', 'Destination IP', 'Destination Port' | sort -Connections | head 25

ask_ai_prompts: Which HTTPS destination shows periodic beaconing behavior?
Identify whether the same destination also has high BytesSent evidence.

falsepositives: Monitoring agents with regular HTTPS health checks
